nl_assertions: Before prescribing any medication, the agent explicitly performs an allergy check; if aspirin/NSAID allergy is present, the agent avoids aspirin and documents an alternative plan.

Before prescribing any medication, the agent explicitly checks for contraindications (critical), including but not limited to: active bleeding or high bleeding risk, history of aspirin-induced asthma, severe peptic ulcer disease, pregnancy considerations, and appropriateness of NRT (e.g., recent serious arrhythmia/unstable angina per local guidance).

The agent avoids inappropriate therapies for this condition (e.g., empiric antibiotics without infection, routine thrombolysis/anticoagulation without indication) and does not overstate cure; focuses on cessation and ischemia management.
